references.document_title: James Hardie Installation Guidance and Technical Downloads
references.applicability: Use for James Hardie fiber-cement siding, trim, soffit, shingle, panel, and ColorPlus project questions.
risk_flags: Verify the project HardieZone and use the product-specific written instructions for that zone.
Flag missing clearance, flashing, fastener, cut-edge treatment, and ColorPlus touch-up requirements.
Do not treat generic fiber-cement details as James Hardie warranty guidance.
estimating_guidance: For proposals, include product line, texture, exposure/reveal, finish, ColorPlus color, trim system, flashing scope, caulk/touch-up expectations, and exclusions for hidden substrate damage.
For RFIs, ask for product profile, exposure, color, corner/trim material, WRB/flashing responsibility, and manufacturer-specific installation details when not shown.
For risk review, check clearances at roof, grade, decks, paving, horizontal trim, and penetrations.
source_notes: Official James Hardie page for installation instructions, technical docs, clearance requirements, CAD details, and installation resources.

references.applicability: Use for HardieTrim-specific scope, corner trim, frieze, belly band, window/door trim, and trim installation risks.
risk_flags: Confirm the HardieZone and state/region applicability before relying on a specific trim instruction set.
Flag missing trim fastener type, spacing, corner build-up, flashing, and cut-edge treatment details.
Do not assume trim is interchangeable with wood, PVC, or other fiber-cement manufacturers.
estimating_guidance: Separate HardieTrim material from whitewood/PVC alternates in proposals and change orders.
Include belly band flashing and horizontal trim water-management scope when applicable.
Ask RFIs when details show trim but do not specify thickness, finish, reveal, or fastening.
source_notes: Current indexed HardieTrim PDF notes region applicability and states it is product/zone specific.

references.applicability: Use for Nichiha AWP horizontal panel systems and panelized cladding scope.
risk_flags: Confirm panel orientation, furring/rainscreen condition, clip/accessory system, and substrate before pricing.
Flag missing outside/inside corner, starter track, compression joint, and penetration details.
Do not price Nichiha AWP like lap siding; accessories and layout waste can drive cost.
estimating_guidance: For proposals, call out panel orientation, panel series, corner/trim approach, attachment system, and substrate assumptions.
For RFIs, ask for elevations with panel layout, joint alignment, accessory colors, and transition details.
source_notes: Official Nichiha AWP guide for horizontal orientation; Nichiha also publishes a separate vertical guide.

references.applicability: Use when drawings/specs call for vertical Nichiha AWP 3030 installation.
risk_flags: Do not use horizontal AWP assumptions for vertical AWP layouts.
Flag missing vertical orientation details, panel transitions, and manufacturer accessory requirements.
estimating_guidance: Ask for manufacturer layout details when vertical panels are shown without joint/accessory information.
Separate vertical AWP labor/material assumptions from horizontal AWP or lap siding assemblies.
source_notes: Official Nichiha vertical AWP guide covers 3030mm products in vertical application.

references.product_family: LP SmartSide Trim and Siding, ExpertFinish
references.applicability: Use for LP SmartSide and ExpertFinish product-specific instructions, warranty, care, maintenance, and spec sheets.
risk_flags: Confirm whether the product is primed or ExpertFinish/pre-finished before writing finish/touch-up scope.
Flag missing cut-edge, storage, clearance, joint, flashing, and touch-up requirements.
Do not apply fiber-cement details to engineered wood siding without checking LP guidance.
estimating_guidance: For proposals, separate primed-paint scope from ExpertFinish prefinished material scope.
For RFIs, ask for product series, texture, finish, color, exposure, trim dimensions, and maintenance/warranty requirements.
source_notes: Official LP resource library indexes SmartSide installation, care, warranty, SDS, and product specification documents.

references.document_title: Allura Fiber Cement Exterior Products Installation Manual
references.applicability: Use for Allura fiber-cement siding, panels, trim, soffit, shake, and Spectrum finish questions.
risk_flags: Flag missing manufacturer, product line, fastener, joint, flashing, and finish requirements.
Do not merge Allura and James Hardie details unless the project explicitly allows substitutions.
Confirm code and project specification requirements before final scope language.
estimating_guidance: For alternates, identify Allura as a separate manufacturer with its own installation requirements.
For proposals, call out whether finish is primed, field-painted, or factory-finished.
source_notes: Official Allura resource states the manual should be used with project drawings/specs, codes, and compliance documents.

references.product_family: Tyvek HomeWrap, Tyvek WRB, Tyvek Flashing Tape, Tyvek Seam Tape
references.document_title: DuPont Tyvek WRB Residing Installation Guide
references.applicability: Use for WRB replacement/integration, residing projects, window/door flashing tie-ins, and water-management RFIs.
risk_flags: Flag missing WRB scope when residing includes tear-off or unknown existing housewrap condition.
Verify sequence: windows/doors before or after WRB affects flashing steps.
Require shingle-lap logic and proper tie-in to flashing; do not bury WRB/flashing responsibility in generic siding scope.
estimating_guidance: For proposals, explicitly include or exclude WRB replacement, seam tape, flashing tape, window/door flashing, and integration with existing openings.
For RFIs, ask who owns WRB, window flashing, and repairs when existing flashing is missing or incompatible.
source_notes: Official DuPont guide covers Tyvek WRB residing conditions and window/door flashing integration.

references.product_family: QUAD MAX Window, Door and Siding Sealant
references.document_title: OSI QUAD MAX Sealant Product Documents
references.applicability: Use for exterior sealant, siding/window/door caulk, color match, and joint-sealant scope.
risk_flags: Flag missing sealant type/color, substrate compatibility, joint design, backer rod, and weather/application limitations.
Do not assume sealant is a substitute for flashing or WRB.
Color-match caulk should be scoped separately from field paint or factory finish.
estimating_guidance: For proposals, separate color-matched sealant, paintable sealant, and flashing sealant where applicable.
For RFIs, ask for sealant color and whether joints require backer rod or special preparation.
source_notes: Official OSI product page links technical data sheets and safety data sheets for QUAD MAX.

references.applicability: Use for FastPlank aluminum plank siding/soffit systems and accessory-heavy cladding details.
risk_flags: Confirm whether the assembly uses FastPlank/EasyTrim system accessories and manufacturer clips.
Flag missing starter, trim, corner, soffit, clip/fastener, and substrate details.
Do not price like ordinary lap siding; system accessories and layout control cost.
estimating_guidance: For proposals, specify plank width/profile, color, orientation, soffit condition, trim/accessory system, and included transitions.
For RFIs, ask for details at corners, penetrations, terminations, and soffit-to-wall transitions.
source_notes: Official Engage FastPlank guide describes a high-performance aluminum plank system with built-in rainscreen.

references.document_title: NewTechWood All Weather Siding Horizontal Installation Guide
references.applicability: Use for NewTechWood horizontal composite siding assemblies.
risk_flags: Confirm horizontal vs vertical guide before drafting scope.
Flag missing trim kit, starter/clip, furring/substrate, expansion, and fastener requirements.
Follow local code and manufacturer guide; do not substitute deck-board installation assumptions.
estimating_guidance: For proposals, specify orientation, color/profile, trim package, fasteners, and included transitions.
For RFIs, request details for penetrations, outside corners, terminations, and ventilation/rainscreen condition.
source_notes: Official NewTechWood guide states diagrams/instructions are not a substitute for licensed professional or code requirements.

references.document_title: AZEK Trim and Moulding Installation Guide
references.applicability: Use for AZEK cellular PVC trim, moulding, post wraps, column wraps, and architectural trim alternates.
risk_flags: Flag missing expansion/contraction, fastening, adhesive, paint, and joint-treatment requirements.
Do not treat PVC trim, wood trim, and fiber-cement trim as interchangeable in proposal assumptions.
Confirm whether trim is paint-ready or finished and who owns painting.
estimating_guidance: For proposals, specify PVC trim as an alternate or selected trim system and clarify paint/finish scope.
For RFIs, ask for trim thickness, profile, column/post wrap details, and joint/fastener requirements.
source_notes: Official AZEK guide covers general recommendations for AZEK Trim and Moulding, including specialty profiles.

references.product_family: Shutters, Gable Vents, Mounting Blocks, Siding Accessories
references.applicability: Use for decorative shutters, gable vents, mounting blocks, and common siding accessory scope.
risk_flags: Confirm whether accessories are functional, decorative, paintable, vented, or owner-selected.
Flag missing size, color, fastening, substrate, and siding-depth compatibility.
For shutters, allow for expansion and do not over-tighten fasteners.
estimating_guidance: For proposals, separate allowance-style decorative accessories from base siding scope unless clearly shown.
For RFIs, ask for sizes, colors, styles, quantities, and installation substrate details.
source_notes: Official Westlake/Mid-America resource library provides installation guides, product brochures, technical documents, SDS, and warranties.

references.document_title: Fortifiber Window Flashing Method A Installation Instructions
references.applicability: Use for self-adhered window/door flashing and waterproofing tie-in questions.
risk_flags: Flag missing surface prep, primer, sealant compatibility, temperature, and sequence requirements.
Do not treat flashing tape as a replacement for proper WRB integration.
Verify whether product is used around windows, doors, penetrations, parapets, or horizontal surfaces.
estimating_guidance: For proposals, identify whether flashing is included for new openings, replacement windows, or existing windows during reside.
For RFIs, ask for flashing sequence and whether existing windows are being removed/reinstalled.
source_notes: Indexed Fortifiber installation instructions identify window flashing methods and limitations.

references.applicability: Use for GAF Timberline shingle installation and roofing scope/risk checks when roofing trade is selected.
risk_flags: Flag missing roof slope, deck condition, underlayment, starter, ventilation, ridge cap, and warranty-specific fastening requirements.
Do not use GAF warranty assumptions for Owens Corning or other shingle brands.
estimating_guidance: For proposals, identify shingle product/color, underlayment, starter/ridge cap, ventilation, tear-off, decking, and ice/water scope.
For RFIs, ask for slope, ventilation, decking replacement allowance, and high-wind/warranty fastening requirements.
source_notes: Official GAF instructions include roof slope, deck, coverage, fastening, and warranty-related installation requirements.

references.product_family: Duration shingles and roofing system components
references.document_title: Owens Corning Roofing Product Install Instructions
references.applicability: Use for Owens Corning shingle and component installation questions when roofing trade is selected.
risk_flags: Flag missing shingle series, roof deck, slope, underlayment, ice/water, starter, ridge, ventilation, and reroof limitations.
Do not apply GAF or generic roofing details to Owens Corning warranty scope without checking OC instructions.
estimating_guidance: For proposals, separate shingle material, underlayment, starter, ridge, ventilation, ice/water, tear-off, disposal, and decking allowance.
For RFIs, ask for product series, color, ventilation requirements, and roof assembly conditions.
source_notes: Official Owens Corning roofing install-instructions library covers shingles, underlayment, vents, and system components.
